# Prosecco.dev — AI Standards Directory

> A curated, open directory of AI agent interoperability standards, protocols, and specifications.
> Maintained by the community to help developers, researchers, and organizations navigate the evolving AI standards landscape.

## About

Prosecco.dev tracks emerging and approved standards for AI agent communication,
tool use, identity, and interoperability. Each standard is categorized by maturity
status (Backlog → Emerging → Draft → Approved), tagged by topic area, and linked
to its primary specification, community channels, and reference implementations.

## Site Map

- Homepage: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/
- Standards Radar (visual overview): https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/radar
- Timeline (activity over time): https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/timeline
- Affiliations (organizations & authors): https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/affiliations
- llms.txt: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/llms.txt
- llms-full.txt: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/llms-full.txt

## Overview

- Total standards tracked: **71**
- Approved: 4
- Draft: 55
- Emerging: 12
- Organizations: 24 (A2A Alliance, AGNTCY, AMP, Agent Manifest Project, Agent Skills, Answer.AI, Anthropic, Catena Labs, Dick Hardt Consulting, Factory, Google, IBM, IETF, IETF Outer Space, IETF Project, IETF Shattered (Individual), Linux Foundation, Open Agent Identity Foundation, OpenTelemetry, Tawk it AI, UTCP Foundation, W3C, Wildcard, Zed)
- Topic tags: A2A, A2G, AI Agent, AI Agents, AI Agents Distributed AI, AI AgentsContext Propagation, AI Identity, AI agents, AI agents破], AP2, API, API Integration, API discovery吹, Access Control, specification, title:, tool calling, transparency, verification破

## Standards Index

- [A Decoupled Authorization Model for Agent2Agent (A2A)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/100ae251-1af5-4270-a292-c249cabff4d3) — Draft · IETF
- [AAuth - Agentic Authorization OAuth 2.1 Extension (AAuth)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2746ec5a-6edc-478a-bd67-d33c7737eca3) — Draft · IETF
- [Agent Attachment Protocol (AAP)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2255d169-51cf-4c29-b1e1-7bf467ce7cf4) — Draft · IETF

- [OAuth2.0 Extention for AI Agent: Authorization on Target (OAuth2-AI-Agent)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2204a9a1-dde9-4330-ab82-64035af40443) — Draft · IETF

- [Policy and Lifecycle Extensions for OAuth Rich Authorization Requests (RAR-AGENT-EXTENSIONS)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/47900c17-640d-4755-86a8-efde0c5aa6e3) — Draft · IETF
- [Problem Statement: Network-Layer Infrastructure for Autonomous Agent Communication (PILOT)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/cb535730-eb66-43d1-8ce8-b0d8a6079d31) — Draft · IETF

- [SD Agent: Selective Disclosure for Agent Discovery and Identity Management (Agent-SD-JWT)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/df514eab-bb74-48c0-9ed6-a87604b927d0) — Draft · IETF
- [Secure Intent Protocol: JWT Compatible Agentic Identity and Workflow Management (Agentic JWT)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/97d78f3a-32f8-4233-b964-59a21e22a8fc) — Draft · IETF
- [Security Requirements for AI Agents (A2A)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/9b98570e-fcd8-44fa-bbd0-32515e9968f1) — Draft · IETF

- [Structured and Constraint Extensions for OAuth Scopes (OAuth Agent Scopes)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/d48d5d5f-614c-4665-986e-9caff90a31c2) — Draft · IETF

- [VCAP: Verified Commerce for Agent Protocols (VCAP)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/8f446234-ae1a-4fbb-964a-799be881e403) — Emerging · IETF

- [x402 Extension for the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A x402)](https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/6127dfb9-dff2-405f-bb01-bbbeee53b247) — Emerging · Google

## Approved Standards

### Agent Manifest — Core Declarative Specification v1.0 (AMCS/v1.0)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/593a86c1-6a78-4776-8452-daa379314658
- Organization: Agent Manifest Project
- Specification: https://agent-manifest-spec.org/spec/v1.0/agent_manifest_v1.0.html
- Tags: autonomous agents, governance, manifest, alignment, transparency, JSON-Schema
- Status: Approved
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A machine-readable, execution-agnostic standard that establishes the minimum declaration requirements for autonomous systems. It defines mandatory structural elements such as identity, purpose, autonomy levels, and stopping authority that must be present before an agent's execution.

---

### Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/23435656-c711-4ee2-9bd1-01fcaeff89cd
- Organization: A2A Alliance
- Specification: https://a2a-protocol.org/
- Tags: Protocol, Agents
- Status: Approved
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

The Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol is a communication standard designed to enable seamless interoperability and interaction between autonomous AI agents. It provides a structured framework for agents to discover, negotiate, and collaborate on tasks across different platforms.

---

### AGENTS.md (AGENTS.md)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/5a9be330-b282-49a0-9269-9f9888240a19
- Organization: Factory
- Specification: https://agents.md/
- Tags: Agents
- Status: Approved
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

AGENTS.md is a simple, open companion to README.md that gives coding agents a predictable, machine-readable place for project guidance (setup commands, test workflows, code style, and PR conventions) reducing README noise. Adopted by 20k+ GitHub repos and portable across tools like Cursor, Aider, RooCode, Zed, and more.

---

### Model Context Protocol (MCP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/e8d35071-696b-417f-b61f-f91bda91a32a
- Organization: Linux Foundation
- Specification: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/
- Tags: Protocol
- Status: Approved
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open protocol that enables seamless integration between LLM applications and external data sources and tools. Whether you're building an AI-powered IDE, enhancing a chat interface, or creating custom AI workflows, MCP provides a standardized way to connect LLMs with the context they need.

---

## Draft Standards

### A Decoupled Authorization Model for Agent2Agent (A2A)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/100ae251-1af5-4270-a292-c249cabff4d3
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-chen-agent-decoupled-authorization-model/00/
- Tags: AI Agents, Authorization, Agent2Agent, Security, Just-in-Time Permissions
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document proposes a framework for dynamic, intent-based authorization for AI Agents. It enables fine-grained, Just-in-Time (JIT) permissions based on an agent's specific intent and behavioral trustworthiness rather than long-lived identities.

---

### AAuth - Agentic Authorization OAuth 2.1 Extension (AAuth)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2746ec5a-6edc-478a-bd67-d33c7737eca3
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-patwhite-aauth-00.html
- Tags: OAuth 2.1, AI Agents, Authorization, Agentic AI, Security
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An OAuth 2.1 extension defining the Agent Authorization Grant for confidential AI agents to request end-user consent and obtain access tokens via polling, SSE, or WebSockets. It introduces natural-language descriptions and a 'reason' parameter specifically for agentic workflows.

---

### Agent Attachment Protocol (AAP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2255d169-51cf-4c29-b1e1-7bf467ce7cf4
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-dunbar-agent-attachment-00
- Tags: autonomous agents, overlay network, agent attachment, identity validation, A2G, Agent Gateway, network protocol
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A network-centric mechanism that enables autonomous agents to securely attach to an overlay network infrastructure. It defines procedures for identity validation, capability exchange, and secure session establishment between agents and network edge nodes.

---

### Agent Auth (AAuth) (AAuth)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/13fc44a1-6e02-4adf-b710-ec6b243516e6
- Organization: Dick Hardt Consulting
- Specification: https://github.com/dickhardt/AAuth/blob/main/draft-hardt-aauth.html
- Tags: AI Agents, Authentication, Authorization, Security, Identity
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

AAuth (Agent Auth) is a protocol designed to enable secure authentication and authorization for autonomous AI agents. It provides a technical framework for agents to prove their identity and obtain permissions to act on behalf of users or organizations.

---

### Agent Authorization Profile (AAP) for OAuth 2.0 (AAP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/01dd5ba2-8887-4957-a42e-4fa091afa4fd
- Organization: IETF Shattered (Individual)
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-aap-oauth-profile/
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, JWT, AI Agents, Authorization, Security, Machine-to-Machine
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An authorization profile for OAuth 2.0 and JWT designed for autonomous AI agents. It extends existing standards with structured claims for agent identity, task context, operational constraints, and human oversight.

---

### Agent Client Protocol (ACP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/06f4f926-c3ca-4880-85a1-9d7dfa999f65
- Organization: Zed
- Specification: https://agentclientprotocol.com/
- Tags: Protocol, Agents
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

The Agent Client Protocol (ACP) is a standardized communication protocol designed to connect AI agents with various client interfaces. It provides a common architecture for session management, initialization, and registry discovery between agents and clients.

---

### Agent Communication Protocol (ACP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/10ca6368-74ec-495c-9f68-aef5d14c050e
- Organization: IBM
- Specification: https://agentcommunicationprotocol.dev/
- Tags: Protocol, Agents
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

The Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) is a standardized messaging and interaction framework designed to enable interoperability between AI agents. It defines a consistent architecture and lifecycle for agent runs, complemented by SDKs for Python and TypeScript.

---

### Agent Directory Service (ADS)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/dd67ac4e-f746-4162-901f-95ab09be6fc7
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-mp-agntcy-ads/
- Tags: AI Agents, Directory Service, Discovery, Metadata, Content Routing, Internet-Draft
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

The Agent Directory Service (ADS) is a distributed directory service designed to store metadata for AI agent applications. It enables the discovery of agents with specific skills through directory records and an interconnected content-routing protocol.

---

### Agent Identity Protocol: Agentic Authentication and Authorized Policy Enforcement (AIP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/1a4e443a-b956-408d-b90d-6b505ec3939b
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-aip-agent-identity-protocol/
- Tags: AI Agents, Identity, Authentication, Security, Policy Enforcement, Cryptography
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An open standard for verifiable identity and policy enforcement for AI agents, providing unique identifiers and cryptographic signing for agent actions. It utilizes a two-layer architecture comprising an identity registry and an enforcement proxy to ensure secure, authorized tool execution.

---

### Agent Message Protocol (AMP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/b2067b9d-2d7b-435f-b89a-b80424911214
- Organization: AMP
- Specification: https://github.com/AMProtocol/AMP/blob/main/validator/spec/v0.3.md
- Tags: AI Agents, Data Economy, Protocol, Validator, Interoperability
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

AMP is an open protocol and registry designed for the AI agent data economy. It provides a specification for validators to ensure the integrity and interoperability of data exchanged between AI agents.

---

### Agent Name Service (ANS): A Universal Directory for Secure AI Agent Discovery and Interoperability (ANS)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/77c981ce-3ab4-481c-b7dc-c6696d977be1
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-narajala-ans-00
- Tags: AI Agents, Discovery, DNS, Interoperability, PKI, Identity
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Agent Name Service (ANS) is a DNS-inspired architecture and universal directory protocol designed for secure AI agent discovery. It provides a protocol-agnostic registry using PKI certificates to verify agent identity and supports multiple communication standards like A2A, MCP, and ACP.

---

### Agent Operation Authorization (AOA)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/45894230-2568-4421-a287-69c6d0f06bcd
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-liu-agent-operation-authorization/02/
- Tags: AI Agents, Authorization, JWT, Security, Delegation, Identity
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A framework that enables verifiable delegation of actions from human principals to autonomous AI agents using structured JWTs. It provides a mechanism for fine-grained authorization of agent operations to prevent unauthorized or hallucinated actions through cryptographic verification.

---

### Agent Payments Protocol (AP2)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/cf2980df-de7f-4869-8a9c-37bd942db9f8
- Organization: Google
- Specification: https://ap2-protocol.org/
- Tags: Protocol, Agents, Payments
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

The Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) facilitates secure, decentralized, and privacy-protecting payments by generative AI agents on behalf of users. It uses Verifiable Digital Credentials (VDCs) to enable interoperability within agentic ecosystems alongside protocols like A2A and MCP.

---

### Agent User Interaction Protocol (AG-UI)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/f7eb3167-8cc0-4162-85a0-217ccbc8d4e9
- Organization: Tawk it AI
- Specification: https://docs.ag-ui.com/
- Tags: Protocol, Agents
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

AG-UI is a lightweight, event-based protocol that standardizes how AI agents connect to user-facing applications. Built for simplicity and flexibility, it enables seamless integration between AI agents, real time user context, and user interfaces.

---

### Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Profile for OAuth Transaction Tokens (A2A)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/a1e59264-8d77-4911-bc01-8c42f3f160d9
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-liu-oauth-a2a-profile/
- Tags: OAuth, Agent-to-Agent, A2A, Transaction Tokens, Identity, Security
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Defines a profile for using OAuth Transaction Tokens in Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication to maintain identity and authorization context across distributed agent workloads. It specifies how call chain context can be embedded within tokens to ensure secure execution flow within trusted domains.

---

### AgentID: An Identity Protocol for Autonomous AI Agents (AgentID)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/9df43f00-5578-4e0f-b859-cc92f9b54f07
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-gudlab-agentid-protocol/
- Tags: AI Agents, Identity, Authentication, JWT, Autonomous Systems
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An open standard for establishing, verifying, and managing the identity of autonomous AI agents using Agent Identity Tokens (AIT). It builds on OAuth 2.0 and JWT to provide accountability, capability claims, and delegation chains for non-human autonomous actors.

---

### AI Agent Discovery and Invocation Protocol (AI-ADI)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/e0d77537-ed3c-4061-8871-e1c0762901e1
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-cui-ai-agent-discovery-invocation/
- Tags: AI Agents, Discovery Protocol, Interoperability, REST API, Multi-Agent Systems
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A standardized protocol for the discovery and invocation of AI agents, defining a common metadata format for agent capabilities and a unified RESTful invocation interface. It aims to facilitate cross-platform interoperability and the formation of multi-agent systems via capability-based matching and intent-based selection.

---

### AI Agent Protocol (AI Agent Protocol)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/1ae45c6f-1736-4e37-bf63-2ae9ae070738
- Organization: W3C
- Specification: https://w3c-cg.github.io/ai-agent-protocol/protocol.html
- Tags: AI Agents, Interoperability, DID, Identity, Decentralized Identifiers, Agent Discovery
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A protocol designed to address interconnection and interoperability between AI agents across different platforms. it focuses on standardized identity verification using Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) and a structured mechanism for agents to publish their capabilities and interfaces.

---

### AI Agent to Tool (A2T) Protocol (A2T)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/6eb253d1-0c47-4f7d-9c61-7fe726198bb3
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-rosenberg-aiproto-a2t/
- Tags: AI Agent, Tool Use, API Integration, Interoperability, LLM
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This protocol facilitates the integration of third-party APIs into AI Agents through two primary features: tool enumeration and tool invocation. It standardizes how enterprise AI agents discover available tools' capabilities and perform inter-domain invocations at run-time.

---

### AIVS: Agentic Integrity Verification Standard (AIVS)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/f034ba27-eff0-4566-9f38-978d6752427b
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-stone-aivs/
- Tags: AI Agents, Cryptography, Integrity Verification, Audit Logs, Attestation
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A portable, self-verifiable archive format for cryptographic proof of AI agent sessions. It uses SHA-256 hash-chained logs and Ed25519 signatures to ensure that agent actions are accounted for, unmodified, and verified offline.

---

### ATEP: Agent Trust and Execution Passport (ATEP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/e1990683-d61a-42af-a4f2-5db3185fd65c
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-stone-atep/
- Tags: AI Agents, Trust, Credentials, Agent-to-Agent Commerce, Verifiable Track Record
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An open standard for representing an AI agent's verifiable track record of work across marketplaces and platforms. It defines a portable, machine-readable credential format encoding execution history, capability domains, and trust tiers computed from append-only logs.

---

### Attenuating Authorization Tokens for Agentic Delegation Chains (AAT)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/222c9073-d40b-42d0-8280-8596559f0e19
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-niyikiza-oauth-attenuating-agent-tokens/
- Tags: OAuth, AI Agents, JWT, Authorization, Delegation, Security
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Defines Attenuating Authorization Tokens (AATs), a JWT-based credential format for secure delegation in AI agent systems. It allows agents to derive more restrictive tokens offline to cryptographically enforce tool-invocation constraints across delegation chains.

---

### Brokered Agent Network for DNS AI Discovery (BANDAID) (BANDAID)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/ab8885e0-80dc-4cf8-b56d-b903a5316513
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-mozleywilliams-dnsop-bandaid/
- Tags: DNS, AI Agents, Service Discovery, Agent-to-Agent, SVCB, DNS-SD, BANDAID
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A method for utilizing the Domain Name System (DNS) to facilitate scalable and interoperable discovery, trust signaling, and metadata exchange between AI agents. It defines a structured DNS namespace and Service Binding (SVCB) record usage model to support capability advertisement without requiring new DNS protocol values.

---

### Clawdentity: Cryptographic Identity and Trust Protocol for AI Agent Communication (Clawdentity)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/3e79771a-4576-4453-9fde-77a5f0648b08
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-ravikiran-clawdentity-protocol-00.html
- Tags: AI agents, identity, cryptographic trust, Ed25519, proof of possession, agent-to-agent
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A cryptographic identity and trust layer for AI agent-to-agent communication. It provides per-agent Ed25519 identity, registry-issued credentials, and bilateral trust establishment via pairing ceremonies.

---

### Considerations for AI Agent Communication and Networking in Enterprise (draft-han-agent-comm-enterprise)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/4a5a2634-8462-44d8-8a33-22be7b0dd97d
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-han-agent-comm-enterprise/
- Tags: AI Agents, Enterprise Networking, Semantic Routing, Agent Discovery, Multi-Agent Cooperation
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document investigates key technologies for AI agent communication in enterprise scenarios, including identification, registration, capability discovery, and secure collaboration. It proposes an agent identifier and semantic routing mechanism to facilitate trusted access and efficient cooperation among heterogeneous agents.

---

### Cross App Access (Identity Assertion Authorization Grant) (XAA)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/80e101b2-e888-4e29-9b52-4aa3b7f7f6ab
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-parecki-oauth-identity-assertion-authz-grant/
- Tags: Agents, Security, OAuth 2.0, Authorization Grant, Token Exchange
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-22

Cross App Access (XAA) lets enterprises enable secure agent-to-app and app-to-app access with centralized IT oversight. Instead of scattered integrations and repeated logins, admins decide what connects, enforce security policies, and see exactly what's being accessed. This makes cross-app and cross-agent integrations seamless and scalable across the enterprise.

---

### Delegated Agent Authorization Protocol (DAAP) (DAAP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/fb46968c-6714-4a82-9a63-757aff85ed58
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-mishra-oauth-agent-grants-00
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, AI Agents, Authorization, Decentralized Identifiers (DID), Delegated Authority, JWT
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A model-neutral, framework-agnostic protocol for verifying that a human authorized a specific AI agent to perform actions on their behalf. It utilizes Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs) for agent identity and provides a tamper-evident audit trail for agent activities.

---

### Digital Identity Management for AI Agent Communication Protocols (Agent ID Requirements)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/e4639a0a-472c-41d3-9429-53b098e6ae56
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-yl-agent-id-requirements-00.html
- Tags: AI Agents, Digital Identity, Communication Protocols, Identity Management, Interoperability
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A specification defining the requirements for digital identity management within AI agent communication protocols. It aims to establish a secure, interoperable foundation for connecting agents with users, tools, and other entities in future networks.

---

### DNS-Native AI Agent Naming and Resolution (DN-ANR)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2ea01819-3921-4954-8164-3ad0e5ae6495
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-cui-dns-native-agent-naming-resolution/
- Tags: AI Agents, DNS, Naming Resolution, DNSSEC, FQDN
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

DN-ANR specifies a system for using Fully Qualified Domain Names (FQDNs) as stable identifiers for AI agents, allowing them to resolve to verifiable endpoints via DNSSEC. It provides minimal indexing capabilities to be used by upper-layer discovery systems without carrying heavy semantic metadata in DNS.

---

### Enhanced A2A Requirements for Agents Collobration in Enterprise (A2A)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/59e1239d-148f-4adf-94e7-1caf472b0e04
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-zgsgl-dispatch-a2a-requirements-enterprise/
- Tags: Agent-to-Agent, A2A, Enterprise AI, Agent Collaboration, Protocol Requirements
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document proposes enhanced requirements for the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol specifically tailored for enterprise applications. It focuses on facilitating collaboration between autonomous agents within an enterprise environment.

---

### Entity Attestation Token (EAT) Profile for Autonomous AI Agents (EAT-AI)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/8f21739a-98a6-4808-b8f6-fb25363c5d14
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-messous-eat-ai/
- Tags: AI Agents, Attestation, Security, CBOR, JWT, Model Integrity, Provenance
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A profile for the Entity Attestation Token (EAT) to support remote attestation of autonomous AI agents. It specifies standardized claims for attesting AI model integrity, data provenance, and inference-time access policies using CBOR or JSON Web Tokens.

---

### Framework for AI Agent Networks (ANP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/15a4bfea-0643-4400-a4ad-aa5572c46420
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-zyyhl-agent-networks-framework/
- Tags: AI Agents, Agent Network Protocol, Networking, Interoperability
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document defines a framework for AI agent networks based on the Agent Network Protocol (ANP). It specifies the basic functions required to support AI agent communication within a trusted domain.

---

### Further considerations on AI Agent Authentication and Authorization Based on OAuth Extension

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2a3af462-941c-4ef9-b6c2-c1480cf2987c
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-yao-agent-auth-considerations/
- Tags: AI Agents, Authentication, Authorization, OAuth Extension, Agent Communication Network
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document proposes extensions to the OAuth model and new workflows designed for AI agent authentication and authorization to support scalable and trustable Agent Communication Networks (ACN). It addresses the specific requirements for verifying identities and permissions of autonomous AI agents within vertical industries.

---

### HTTP Message Signatures for automated traffic Architecture (Web Bot Auth Architecture)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/a54cd698-10cb-4272-ba70-88ae0c3bcd39
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-meunier-web-bot-auth-architecture
- Tags: HTTP, Security, Authentication, Bot Detection, Cryptography, Message Signatures
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An architecture for identifying and authenticating automated HTTP traffic using cryptographic message signatures. It allows automated clients to sign outbound requests so that servers can verify their identity with high confidence.

---

### OAuth 2.0 Extension: On-Behalf-Of User Authorization for AI Agents (OAuth 2.0 AI Agent Extension)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/fd971cf8-8dca-432b-b351-1d00dd41909b
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-oauth-ai-agents-on-behalf-of-user/
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, AI Agents, Authorization, Delegation, Identity
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This specification extends the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework to enable AI agents to securely obtain access tokens for acting on behalf of users. It introduces parameters to identify the specific agent requiring delegation and ensures secure delegation with explicit user consent and enhanced auditability.

---

### OAuth 2.0 Scope Aggregation for Multi-Step AI Agent Workflows

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/ddb6b423-2983-424b-abd1-36d2ce35d6d0
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-jia-oauth-scope-aggregation
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, AI Agents, Authorization, Scope Aggregation, Workflows
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An OAuth 2.0 authorization pattern that allows AI agents to aggregate multiple required scopes into a single authorization procedure. This approach reduces repeated user consent prompts and improves efficiency in multi-step AI agent workflows across different resources.

---

### OAuth Entity Profiles for Model-Oriented Resource Access (MORA) (MORA)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/11a2e753-9a82-477e-83c7-60f9c06fd3cf
- Organization: Anthropic
- Specification: https://github.com/Sreyanth/draft-mora-oauth-entity-profiles
- Tags: OAuth, AI Identity, Model Access Control, MORA, Authentication
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

OAuth Entity Profiles for Model-Oriented Resource Access (MORA) establishes a framework for identifying and authenticating different entities (like AI models and developers) within an OAuth flow. It specifically addresses how AI models can be uniquely identified and authorized to access resources.

---

### OAuth Identity and Authorization Chaining Across Domains (OAuth Identity Chaining)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/d9156e41-2b8a-496f-9577-2766de5c6b41
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-oauth-identity-chaining-05
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, Identity, Authorization, Security, Trust Domains, Identity Chaining
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This specification defines a mechanism to preserve identity and authorization information across trust domains that use the OAuth 2.0 Framework. It enables secure propagation of user context and permissions through multiple interconnected domains.

---

### OAuth2.0 Extension for Multi-AI Agent Collaboration (OAuth2.0-AI-Agent)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/59ead549-5128-48cb-9de8-dcd84c088a0b
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-song-oauth-ai-agent-collaborate-authz
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, AI Agents, Multi-Agent Collaboration, Authorization, Agentic AI
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An extension to the OAuth 2.0 protocol designed for task-oriented multi-AI agent collaboration. It enables a lead agent to coordinate sub-agents as a task group by adding specific fields in token and message flows, streamlining the authorization process.

---

### OAuth2.0 Extention for AI Agent: Authorization on Target (OAuth2-AI-Agent)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2204a9a1-dde9-4330-ab82-64035af40443
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-song-oauth-ai-agent-authorization/
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, AI Agent, Authorization, Security, Identity
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An extension to the OAuth 2.0 protocol that introduces a target_id field for granular authorization of AI agents. It aims to support precise permission management and enhance traceability for virtual or physical AI components in a network.

---

### Open Agent Identity Specification (OAI-1)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/b4f58ed2-3635-40b9-9e0f-4ed687e873d7
- Organization: Open Agent Identity Foundation
- Specification: https://openagentidentity.org
- Tags: identity, discovery, AI agents, decentralized identity constellations, interoperability, DNS, MCP
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A decentralized standard for discovering, verifying, and interacting with AI agents using DNS-rooted identity. It provides a universal identity foundation that enables interoperability between different agent protocols like MCP, A2A, and UCP.

---

### Policy and Lifecycle Extensions for OAuth Rich Authorization Requests (RAR-AGENT-EXTENSIONS)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/47900c17-640d-4755-86a8-efde0c5aa6e3
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-chen-oauth-rar-agent-extensions-00
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, RAR, AI Agents, Authorization, Security, Lifecycle Management
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Extends OAuth 2.0 Rich Authorization Requests (RAR) with policy context and lifecycle binding to support AI agent ecosystems. It introduces mechanisms to request specific security assurance levels and to bind authorization validity to the lifecycle of automated tasks.

---

### Problem Statement: Network-Layer Infrastructure for Autonomous Agent Communication (PILOT)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/cb535730-eb66-43d1-8ce8-b0d8a6079d31
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-teodor-pilot-problem-statement/
- Tags: Autonomous Agents, Network Infrastructure, Agent Communication, AI Identity, Internet-Draft
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document describes the requirement for a network-layer infrastructure that provides autonomous agents with first-class network citizenship. It addresses the limitations of current agent communication protocols that rely on centralized application-layer infrastructure and lack native network-layer identity, addressing, and transport.

---

### Registry and Signature Agent card for Web bot auth (WebBotAuth Registry)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/09cef729-a711-4460-a8c3-b65f64d0567b
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-meunier-webbotauth-registry/
- Tags: Web Bot, Authentication, JSON, IANA Registry, Signature Agent, Metadata
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document defines a JSON-based 'Signature Agent Card' format for web agents to advertise metadata including identity, purpose, and cryptographic keys. It also establishes an IANA registry for these parameters to enable extensible and interoperable discovery of agent information.

---

### SCIM Agents and Agentic Applications Extension (SCIM Agent Extension)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/4d0fa0cd-4b02-42d6-8fc8-130b2348391d
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://www.ietf.org/archive/id/draft-abbey-scim-agent-extension-00.html
- Tags: SCIM, Identity Management, AI Agents, Agentic Applications, Workloads, Interoperability
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document describes a SCIM 2.0 extension for agents and agentic applications, including extensions to core User and Group objects and new resource types. It aims to provide interoperability between identity providers and agentic constructs while reducing the need for new specialized protocols.

---

### SD Agent: Selective Disclosure for Agent Discovery and Identity Management (Agent-SD-JWT)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/df514eab-bb74-48c0-9ed6-a87604b927d0
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-nandakumar-agent-sd-jwt-02
- Tags: SD-JWT, A2A, Agent Discovery, Identity Management, Privacy, Model Context Protocol
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Integrates Selective Disclosure for JWTs (SD-JWT) with Agent-to-Agent (A2A) systems to provide privacy-preserving agent discovery and identity management. It specifies the SD-Card format to enable selective disclosure of agent capabilities, contact information, and metadata.

---

### Secure Intent Protocol: JWT Compatible Agentic Identity and Workflow Management (Agentic JWT)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/97d78f3a-32f8-4233-b964-59a21e22a8fc
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-goswami-agentic-jwt/
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, JWT, AI Agents, Identity, Zero-Trust, Security, Agentic AI
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An extension to OAuth 2.0 and JWT designed to address authorization challenges unique to autonomous agentic AI systems. It introduces cryptographic agent identity through checksums and workflow-aware token binding to ensure alignment between user intent and autonomous agent actions.

---

### Security Requirements for AI Agents (A2A)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/9b98570e-fcd8-44fa-bbd0-32515e9968f1
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ni-a2a-ai-agent-security-requirements/
- Tags: AI Agents, Security Requirements, Agent-to-Agent, Identity, Access Control
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This document specifies security requirements for AI agents across various interaction stages including provisioning, registration, discovery, and cross-domain interconnection. It focuses on establishing a secure framework for agent-to-agent (A2A) communication and access control.

---

### Semantic Conventions for GenAI agent and framework spans (OTEL)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/37b22337-7024-4fd6-87bc-f00d35a8a18b
- Organization: OpenTelemetry
- Specification: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-agent-spans/
- Tags: GenAI, Observability, Telemetry, Semantic Conventions, Tracing, LLM, Agents
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Defines a standardized set of telemetry attributes for Generative AI applications, specifically focusing on agent and framework-level spans. It provides a transition plan for instrumentations to opt-in to experimental GenAI conventions while maintaining backwards compatibility.

---

### Structured and Constraint Extensions for OAuth Scopes (OAuth Agent Scopes)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/d48d5d5f-614c-4665-986e-9caff90a31c2
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-chen-oauth-scope-agent-extensions
- Tags: OAuth 2.0, AI Agents, Authorization, Security, Scopes, Fine-grained Access Control
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Defines a structured format for OAuth 2.0 scopes to support fine-grained permissions for AI Agent skills and modular capability units. It introduces a colon-separated syntax for specifying resource types, actions, targets, and constraints.

---

### System for Cross-domain Identity Management: Agentic Identity Schema (SCIM-AGENT-SCHEMA)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/2e27898a-7ae7-4636-bad2-97fc20c67f78
- Organization: IETF Project
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-wahl-scim-agent-schema/
- Tags: AI Agents, Identity Management, SCIM, JSON Schema, Authentication
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Provides a platform-neutral schema for representing AI agents' identities in JSON format within the System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM). It enables agentic identities to be transferred, authenticated, and authorized to interact with services.

---

### The agent:// Protocol -- A URI-Based Framework for Interoperable Agents (agent://)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/f6149ce7-9098-4563-8fda-4330d1f0a549
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-narvaneni-agent-uri/02/
- Tags: AI Agents, URI, Interoperability, Protocol, Orchestration
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A URI template-based framework for addressing, invoking, and interoperating with autonomous and semi-autonomous software agents. It provides a layered architecture for capability discovery, contracts, and orchestration across different agent ecosystems.

---

### Transaction Tokens For Agents (OTTA)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/448f9713-fef5-4ffe-bc9d-21d19445bbf4
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-oauth-transaction-tokens-for-agents/
- Tags: OAuth, Security, AI AgentsContext Propagation, Transaction Tokens, Authentication
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An extension to the OAuth Transaction Tokens framework to support agent context propagation. It defines 'actor' and 'principal' fields to allow services to distinguish between the agent performing an action and the entity that initiated it.

---

### Universal Tool Calling Protocol (UTCP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/d9494392-fa34-4043-be01-6511f9886e84
- Organization: UTCP Foundation
- Specification: https://utcp.io/about/RFC
- Tags: tool calling, AI agents, interoperability吹, API discovery吹, decentralized
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A decentralized protocol that enables AI agents to discover and use external tools by interacting with them directly via their native protocols. It eliminates the 'wrapper tax' by using a standardized 'Manual' (JSON file) to describe tools and their transport requirements without requiring changes to the underlying APIs.

---

### WIMSE Applicability for AI Agents (WIMSE AI Agent Identity)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/9f216bbc-9212-43e4-95f3-7c7c06064a5f
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ni-wimse-ai-agent-identity/
- Tags: AI Agents, Identity, WIMSE, Security, Credentials, Agentic AI
- Status: Draft
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This specification discusses the applicability of Workload Identity in Multi-Service Environments (WIMSE) to Agentic AI. It aims to establish independent identities and credential management mechanisms for AI agents.

---

## Emerging Standards

### Agent Commerce Kit (ACK)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/22430209-6944-43e1-be8b-88205543a631
- Organization: Catena Labs
- Specification: https://www.agentcommercekit.com/
- Tags: Protocol, Agents, Security, Payments
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Agent Commerce Kit (ACK) is an open-source toolkit that enables AI agents to securely participate in commerce through two protocols: ACK-ID for verifiable agent identity using W3C DIDs and VCs, and ACK-Pay for standardized agent-native payments across diverse settlement rails.

---

### Agent Skills Specification (Skills)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/83aa1a86-38ec-45d7-8610-a28e77b6aea0
- Organization: Agent Skills
- Specification: https://agentskills.io/specification
- Tags: ai agents, autonomous agents, skills, interoperability, specification
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A format specification for defining and packaging modular capabilities, or skills, for AI agents to enable interoperability across different platforms.

---

### agents.json (agents.json)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/6018424b-7ebf-4193-8d97-3a7febb5b2a3
- Organization: Wildcard
- Specification: https://docs.wild-card.ai/agentsjson/introduction
- Tags: API, Agents, JSON Specification, OpenAPI Extension, Agentic Interoperability
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Agents.json is an open-source JSON specification built on top of the OpenAPI standard that formally describes contracts for API and AI agent interactions. It provides a structured way for agents to understand and interact with diverse service capabilities.

---

### AGNTCY Identity Specification (AGNTCY)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/7c14b85a-1ceb-4568-a5f0-aebd76d3276c
- Organization: AGNTCY
- Specification: https://github.com/agntcy/identity-spec
- Tags: identity, agents, MCP, multi-agent systems破, verification破, AI agents破], title:
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A specification for onboarding, creating, and verifying identities for AI Agents, Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, and Multi-Agent Systems (MASs). It establishes a standard framework for identity management in autonomous agent ecosystems.

---

### AI Agent Authentication and Authorization (AI-AGENT-AUTH)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/e31a4ba1-488f-4a62-843b-d181c8b5e289
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-klrc-aiagent-auth/
- Tags: AI Agents, Authentication, Authorization, OAuth 2.0, WIMSE, Security
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Proposes a model for authentication and authorization of AI agent interactions by leveraging WIMSE and OAuth 2.0. It aims to provide a framework for using existing standards and identifying gaps to guide future standardization for agents.

---

### Cross-Domain Interoperability Framework for AI Agent Collaboration (CDI)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/1cd8d0f6-bd37-43cd-8132-3248de6dde7c
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-cui-dmsc-agent-cdi/
- Tags: AI Agents, Interoperability, Federated Identity, Cross-Domain Collaboration, Multi-Agent Systems
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A framework for enabling seamless cross-domain interoperability among AI agents operating across different networks, administrative domains, and heterogeneous platforms. It specifies mechanisms for agent discovery, capability negotiation, trust delegation, and federated policy enforcement to facilitate scalable multi-domain AI collaboration.

---

### llms.txt (llms.txt)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/1b83a5a6-344d-4910-acc4-c11074236f68
- Organization: Answer.AI
- Specification: https://llmstxt.org/
- Tags: Standard
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

llms.txt is a standardized markdown file that gives LLMs concise, expert-level context and pointers to detailed docs, avoiding noisy HTML. It is human- and machine-readable in a precise format suitable for deterministic parsing (parsers/regex). Sites are encouraged to offer clean markdown versions of pages at the same URL with .md appended (or index.html.md for path URLs) to support reliable ingestion.

---

### The Intent Token: A Cryptographic Authorization Primitive for Autonomous Agents (Intent Token)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/0c97d3a2-5f9d-4caa-b174-2ce76c756686
- Organization: IETF Outer Space
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-williams-intent-token/
- Tags: AI Agents, Authorization, Cryptography, Autonomous Systems, OAuth
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

A cryptographic authorization primitive that binds autonomous agent actions to human-declared authorization envelopes. It fills the gap in existing frameworks by governing what an autonomous agent is authorized to do at the moment of action.

---

### Using LISP as a Network Substrate for AI Agent Communication (LISP-AI-Agent)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/d1290eeb-137e-473e-941b-8dc510c4aa86
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-wang-lisp-ai-agent/
- Tags: Networking, AI Agents Distributed AI, LISP, Protocol
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

Explores how the Locator/ID Separation Protocol (LISP) can serve as a network substrate for distributed AI agents across cloud, edge, and endpoint environments. It outlines extensions to LISP to support context-aware mapping and AI agent-centric communication.

---

### VCAP-AP2 Binding: Verified Commerce Settlement for the Agent Payments Protocol (VCAP-AP2 Binding)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/f670b4b7-aa5b-4461-a6f1-84165767f1b3
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-stone-vcap-ap2-binding/
- Tags: Agent Payments Protocol, AP2, VCAP, AI Agents, Escrow, Commerce Settlement, Cryptographic Proofs
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

This specification defines how the Verified Commerce for Agent Protocols (VCAP) settlement layer binds to the Agent Payments Protocol (AP2). It provides the cryptographic proof bindings and verification mechanisms required for escrow fund release based on delivery confirmation in agent-initiated transactions.

---

### VCAP: Verified Commerce for Agent Protocols (VCAP)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/8f446234-ae1a-4fbb-964a-799be881e403
- Organization: IETF
- Specification: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-stone-vcap/
- Tags: AI Agents, Autonomous Agents, Payments, Settlement Protocol, Proof of Work Delivery, Cryptographic Verification
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

An open standard for settling financial transactions between autonomous AI agents using cryptographically verifiable proof of work delivery. It defines message formats and state machines for holding funds in escrow and releasing payments based on machine-verifiable evidence.

---

### x402 Extension for the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A x402)

- Prosecco page: https://prosecco-ai-standards.lovable.app/standard/6127dfb9-dff2-405f-bb01-bbbeee53b247
- Organization: Google
- Specification: https://github.com/google-agentic-commerce/a2a-x402
- Tags: Protocol, Agents, Payments, Transport
- Status: Emerging
- Last updated: 2026-03-23

The A2A x402 Extension enables cryptocurrency payments within the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol. It provides a mechanism for agents to monetize services through on-chain payments, reviving the concept of HTTP 402 'Payment Required' for decentralized AI agents.

---
